data.title: Bitcoin's 46% drop not attributed to quantum fears, as per developer Matt Carallo, dismisses blaming them for sluggish price.
data.description: According to Bitcoin developer Matt Carallo, individuals involved in Bitcoin are searching for a scapegoat for the cryptocurrency's slow price growth. Carallo has brushed off concerns related to quantum technology as the cause of this issue.

data.title: Henriette Mersebach will step down as ALK's head of R&D on 23 Feb 2026, will support transition and handover.
data.description: Today, ALK (ALKB:DC / Nasdaq Copenhagen: ALK B) shared that Henriette Mersebach will resign as Executive Vice President of Research and Development and as a Board of Management member, effective February 23, 2026. She will provide assistance during the transition period as required before stepping down.

data.title: Brunel Q4 2025 results: Revenue of EUR 300.4M (down 10%) & Gross profit of EUR 52.8M (down 15%). Underlying EBIT EUR 11.6M (down 20%). Cost savings of EUR 20M implemented. Full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Brunel International N.V. announced its Q4 2025 and full year results, with revenue of EUR 300.4 million in Q4, down 10% YoY, and EUR 1,217.7 million for FY 2025, down 11% YoY. The gross profit was EUR 52.8 million in Q4 (down 15% YoY) and EUR 218.1 million for FY 2025 (down 17% YoY). Underlying EBIT was EUR 11.6 million in Q4 (down 20% YoY) and EUR 38.2 million for FY 2025 (down 35% YoY). Operating costs were reduced to EUR 41.2 million in Q4 and EUR 179.9 million for FY 2025. A cost reduction program was fully implemented, with a new program launched in Q4 for additional savings of EUR 10 million. Total one-off restructuring costs for 2025 were not provided. The company is reinvesting some savings in sales and recruitment capabilities.

data.title: ALK reports 15% revenue growth and 26% EBIT margin in 2025, expects high growth in 2026, and recommends a DKK 355M dividend payout.
data.description: ALK's full-year financial results surpassed expectations, with revenues at the upper end of the projected range. Strong performance in Q4 contributed to this outcome, indicating ongoing commercial success. ALK anticipates a robust organic revenue growth of 11-15% and increased earnings for the year 2026. The company aims for a profitability margin of around 25%, aligning with its established long-term objectives. In accordance with this performance, the Board of Directors has proposed a dividend payout of DKK 355 million for 2025, representing roughly 30% of net profit after taxes, as a direct reward to shareholders.

data.title: Indonesia and Trump agree on trade deal, setting tariffs at 19%. Countries commit to export control cooperation.
data.description: Indonesia and another country recently finalized an agreement that was initially struck last year. As per the terms, Indonesia will sidestep a tariff increase and has pledged to collaborate on export regulations.

data.title: The Royal Ballet School in LONDON offers free workshops to 160 Dagenham primary school students, Years 3, 4, and 5.
data.description: On the 14th of February, The Royal Ballet School organized a series of complimentary workshops for students from nearby schools in Dagenham. Approximately 160 students in Years 3, 4, and 5 participated in these insight workshops aimed at familiarizing them with the School’s national Associate Programme. The workshops were conducted by Victoria Collinson, the Head of Associates and Primary Steps Programmes at The Royal Ballet School, with assistance from Emily Gibbs. The event provided local school children the opportunity to engage with the School’s offerings and potentially explore further involvement in the Associate Programme.
